narration: You set up autopay to simplify life. But that green checkmark just switched off your brain's spending alarm — and it's costing you $1,700 a year.
visual: Smartphone showing autopay list with green checkmarks animating in one by one (Netflix, gym, cloud storage), then a bold red '$1,700' cost badge springs in at the bottom

narration: Scientists call it the pain of payment. Every manual payment fires a loss signal in your brain. Autopay shuts that signal off completely.
visual: Brain SVG with a lightning bolt pain signal on light background, flanked by two meter bars: Manual Pay (full pain) vs Autopay (meter dropping to zero as animation plays)

narration: The average household has 7 forgotten autopay charges — gyms you never visit, apps you never open, billing you every single month.
visual: 7 subscription cards animating in on dark background in a grid: gym, streaming, cloud, music, news app, VPN, fitness app — each with a price label

narration: Companies love autopay customers. They raise prices knowing you won't notice. Netflix, Spotify, and Amazon Prime all hiked rates while autopay users stayed quiet.
visual: Bar chart on light background: 3 company bars (Netflix, Spotify, Amazon) with gray base and red extension showing price increase, plus percentage badges

narration: Do the math: seven charges at $20 average is $140 every month. Multiply by twelve — that's $1,680, almost $1,700 silently gone from your account.
visual: Animated math equation on dark background: 7 × $20 = counter reaching $140/mo, then × 12 = counter reaching $1,680, total badge at bottom

narration: Open your bank app right now, find every autopay charge, and cancel just two of them today. That one move puts $1,700 back in your pocket.
visual: Three-step action checklist on light background: 1. Open bank app (phone icon), 2. Find autopay charges (magnifying glass), 3. Cancel two today (X button). Pulsing red CTA.
